title: Fresenius Kabi Agilia Infusion Pump - Occlusion or Line Blockage Alarms
description: Guide for troubleshooting occlusion or line blockage alarms on a Fresenius Kabi Agilia infusion pump.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop the infusion and move the patient to another pump if necessary. Do not troubleshoot while the line is connected to an active patient.

steps.title: Inspect the Infusion Line
steps.instructions: Check the tubing for kinks, bends, or compression. Ensure clamps are open and lines are not trapped under rails or bedding.

steps.title: Check Syringe or Bag Position
steps.instructions: Confirm that the syringe or infusion bag is seated correctly. Misalignment can trigger occlusion alarms.

steps.title: Verify Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure the catheter, extension sets, and pump tubing are fully connected. Loose or partially connected lines can cause pressure errors.

steps.title: Examine the Filter or Luer Lock
steps.instructions: Some lines have inline filters or connectors that can block flow if obstructed. Replace filters if visibly blocked.

steps.title: Check for Viscous or Particulate Solutions
steps.instructions: Highly viscous fluids may trigger alarms. Consider adjusting flow rate or using a larger gauge line if appropriate (per protocol).

steps.title: Restart Pump
steps.instructions: Power cycle the pump to clear transient error messages. Confirm that alarms do not recur after reconnection with corrected setup.

CCR.Complaint: Pump keeps alarming for occlusion despite correct flow setup.
CCR.Cause: Observed tubing kink under mattress; inline filter partially blocked.
CCR.Resolution: Straightened tubing, replaced filter, and restarted pump. Alarm cleared, pump returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested: Ensure power source stable.
Tubing checked: Kinks, clamps, and connection points.
Alarm type: Occlusion detected, pressure threshold exceeded.
Flow rate: Normal for the therapy prescribed.
Final device status: Pump returned to service or removed if unresolved.

title: Fresenius Kabi Agilia Infusion Pump - Battery Failure / Won’t Power On
description: Troubleshoot Fresenius Kabi Agilia infusion pump that will not power on or has a dead battery.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not attempt to troubleshoot while the pump is connected to a patient. Transfer the patient to a functioning pump before performing any checks.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Confirm that the AC power adapter is plugged into a working outlet. Test the outlet with another device to ensure power is present. Reason: Sometimes the issue is simply no external power, not the pump or battery.

steps.title: Inspect Power Adapter and Cable
steps.instructions: Check for visible damage to the adapter, cable, and connectors. Ensure the connector seats properly in the pump. Reason: Damaged cables are a common cause of battery not charging.

steps.title: Check Battery Installation
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat the battery according to manufacturer instructions. Inspect for corrosion or damage on contacts. Reason: Poor battery contact can prevent charging or powering on.

steps.title: Test with AC Power Only
steps.instructions: Attempt to power the pump directly from AC without the battery installed. Reason: Isolates whether the battery is the cause.

steps.title: Check Battery Charge Level
steps.instructions: If a removable battery is present, ensure it has adequate charge or try a known-good battery if available. Reason: Batteries degrade over time and may fail to hold a charge.

steps.title: Observe Indicator Lights
steps.instructions: Note any LED or display behavior when connecting power or pressing the power button. Reason: Some lights indicate charging status or internal faults.

CCR.Complaint: Pump will not power on; display remains blank.
CCR.Cause: External power and battery verified; battery contacts clean; pump does not power on with AC supply—likely internal battery module or electronics failure.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent to repair vendor.

helpfulDetails: Power cable inspected and confirmed functional

title: Fresenius Kabi Agilia Infusion Pump - Door or Cartridge Not Detected
description: Troubleshoot Fresenius Kabi Agilia infusion pump that fails to detect the syringe or cartridge, or reports a door/cover issue.

steps.title: Ensure the pump is powered on
steps.instructions: Check battery or power connection. A low or dead battery can prevent the pump from detecting the cartridge. This ensures the pump electronics are active.

steps.title: Inspect the cartridge
steps.instructions: Verify that the cartridge is the correct type and size for the Agilia pump. Look for damage, leaks, or misalignment of the plunger. Incorrect cartridges may not engage sensors properly.

steps.title: Check cartridge installation
steps.instructions: Ensure the cartridge is fully seated in the pump holder. Listen for a click or feel for proper engagement. Misalignment often triggers the detection error.

steps.title: Verify door or cover closure
steps.instructions: Ensure the pump’s cartridge cover or door is completely closed. Sensors may not detect the cartridge if the door is partially open.

steps.title: Inspect for debris or obstruction
steps.instructions: Check the cartridge compartment for dust, fluids, or foreign objects. Even small particles can block sensor engagement.

steps.title: Clean sensor and contact areas
steps.instructions: Use a soft, dry cloth or approved wipe to clean any visible sensors or contacts. Dirt or residue can prevent the pump from recognizing the cartridge.

steps.title: Reset the pump
steps.instructions: Power cycle the device after verifying cartridge installation and door closure. This can recalibrate sensors and clear false detection errors.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displays “Cartridge Not Detected” and will not start infusion.
CCR.Cause: Cartridge not fully seated, door partially closed, or sensor blocked by debris.
CCR.Resolution: Verified cartridge type and seating, cleaned compartment, ensured door fully closed, and power-cycled the pump. Pump now detects cartridge and operates normally.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested or battery status checked
Final device status (functional or sent for repair)

title: Fresenius Kabi Agilia Infusion Pump - Keypad / Button Unresponsive
description: Guide for troubleshooting unresponsive keypad/buttons on Fresenius Kabi Agilia infusion pumps, focusing on external checks before internal repair.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Check that the pump is properly connected to a known working AC outlet or that the battery is sufficiently charged. Why: A low or intermittent power supply can cause unresponsive buttons.

steps.title: Inspect the Keypad Surface
steps.instructions: Ensure buttons are not stuck, wet, or obstructed by debris. Why: Physical obstructions or moisture can prevent proper button contact.

steps.title: Restart the Pump
steps.instructions: Power cycle the pump by turning it off, waiting 10 seconds, and powering it back on. Why: Temporary software glitches can sometimes cause unresponsive inputs.

steps.title: Check for Locked or Inactive Mode
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump is not in a locked state or a mode that disables certain buttons (e.g., programming lock). Why: Some Agilia pumps lock the keypad to prevent accidental changes.

steps.title: Test with Another User/Setup
steps.instructions: If possible, move the pump to a different outlet and try different programming sequences. Why: Eliminates environmental or operator error as a factor.

steps.title: Observe for Error Codes or Alarms
steps.instructions: Look for any keypad-related error messages on the display that may indicate software or hardware issues.

CCR.Complaint: Keypad unresponsive; cannot enter infusion parameters.
CCR.Cause: Observed no button response; power verified; keypad free of debris; pump restarted with no effect.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from service; labeled Out of Service; sent for bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested with a known device
Pump restarted and error messages noted
Physical inspection of keypad for damage, debris, or moisture

title: Fresenius Kabi Agilia Infusion Pump - Infusion Programming or Rate Errors
description: Troubleshooting Fresenius Kabi Agilia infusion pump when incorrect programming or infusion rate errors occur.

steps.title: Verify Power and Battery
steps.instructions: Ensure the pump is powered on and the battery or AC source is functioning. Why: A weak battery or intermittent power can cause programming errors or missed inputs.

steps.title: Confirm Correct Syringe/Cartridge Installation
steps.instructions: Check that the syringe or cartridge matches the pump’s size and type specifications. Ensure it is properly seated and the door is closed. Why: The pump calculates rates based on syringe size; wrong size triggers errors.

steps.title: Check Infusion Settings
steps.instructions: Review programmed rate, volume, and mode for correctness. Confirm units (mL/h) and duration match the intended prescription. Why: Simple data entry mistakes are common and easy to correct.

steps.title: Inspect Tubing and Connections
steps.instructions: Verify there are no kinks, occlusions, or clamped lines. Ensure the line is compatible with the pump. Why: Flow restrictions can cause the pump to alarm or incorrectly calculate rates.

steps.title: Reset the Pump (Soft Reset)
steps.instructions: Power cycle the pump following manufacturer guidance. Reprogram the infusion and test with a known empty syringe first. Why: Some errors can be cleared with a simple reset, avoiding unnecessary escalation.

steps.title: Check for Software or Firmware Alerts
steps.instructions: Ensure the pump is up to date and not showing software error messages. Why: Outdated firmware can cause miscalculations in rate delivery.

CCR.Complaint: Pump displays incorrect infusion rate despite correct programming.
CCR.Cause: Pump settings confirmed; syringe type correct; no occlusions found; error persisted after power cycle.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for vendor repair.
